commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4604c64cd532dc5591ab1f33cf3b4c525e263f6f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the OpenAI/ChatGPT Responses replay converters and tests so encrypted reasoning replay items retain their `rs_*` id while other store-disabled replay ids remain omitted, and it also modifies `CHANGELOG.md`.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests 0, Docs 0. Total +10 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the malformed payload shape: current main combines `store=false`, `include: ["reasoning.encrypted_content"]`, and unconditional reasoning id deletion, with tests asserting that bad shape. No live backend reproduction was established in this review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor/maintainer review: remove the changelog edit and provide or waive live backend proof; ClawSweeper should not queue automated repair while external real behavior proof is missing.

target.title: fix(openai): preserve encrypted reasoning replay ids
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes encrypted reasoning replay on the native ChatGPT subscription / `openai-chatgpt-responses` path.

- Preserve the `rs_*` Responses item id when a replayed reasoning item still carries `reasoning.encrypted_content`.
- Keep the existing `store=false` behavior for ordinary message and function-call replay ids.
- Update the native ChatGPT provider test that previously reinforced the bad payload shape.
- This is not a runtime-selection change: the Pi/OpenClaw direct path can already reach this provider; the bug was in replay payload construction.

Why this matters now?

Issue #90093 reports second-turn `invalid_encrypted_content` failures after a first turn succeeds. The current payload builder could replay a reasoning item as encrypted ciphertext without its original Responses item id, which is neither a valid full replay item nor a clean no-reasoning replay.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to runtime selection, Codex app-server routing, or `agentRuntime.id` normalization.
- No broad disabling of encrypted reasoning replay.
- No change to retry stripping behavior for actually stale/invalid encrypted content.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether encrypted reasoning `id` preservation should apply in both converters touched here:
  - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`
  - `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts`
- Whether preserving ids only when `encrypted_content` is present is the right boundary for `store=false` requests.

## Linked context

Closes #90093

Related #90083
Related #88451

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request. This follows investigation in #90093 and the maintainer-visible issue comment with the narrowed failure shape.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  - Native ChatGPT / `openai-chatgpt-responses` replay could send a prior reasoning item with `encrypted_content` but without its original `rs_*` `id`.
  - That malformed middle state matches the reported second-turn `invalid_encrypted_content` failure in #90093.

- Real environment tested:
  - Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS/Darwin arm64.
  - Node `v24.15.0`.
  - Dependencies hydrated with `npx pnpm@latest install --frozen-lockfile`.
  - Real OpenClaw provider/transport payload builders exercised via Vitest and `streamOpenAICodexResponses(..., { onPayload })`; no mocked replacement converter and no live OpenAI call.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  git diff --check
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts -t "preserves encrypted reasoning item ids" --reporter=verbose
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t "preserves encrypted reasoning ids|keeps embedded replay provenance and encrypted reasoning ids|omits native Codex replay item ids" --reporter=verbose
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --reporter=dot
  ```

- Evidence after fix:

  Focused native ChatGPT provider payload capture passes:

  ```text
  ✓ ../../src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts > streamOpenAICodexResponses transport > preserves encrypted reasoning item ids for store-disabled ChatGPT requests 19ms

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       1 passed | 11 skipped (12)
  ```

  Focused transport coverage passes:

  ```text
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       6 passed | 474 skipped (480)
  ```

  Full changed-test-file run passes:

  ```text
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       12 passed (12)

  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       240 passed (240)

  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 6.30s
  ```

- Observed result after fix:

  The native ChatGPT provider now captures a store-disabled request whose replayed reasoning item keeps both fields together:

  ```json
  {
    "type": "reasoning",
    "id": "rs_prior",
    "encrypted_content": "ciphertext",
    "summary": []
  }
  ```

  while normal replay ids remain omitted for store-disabled messages/function calls:

  ```ts
  expect(messageItem).not.toHaveProperty("id");
  expect(functionCall).not.toHaveProperty("id");
  ```

- What was not tested:

  - No live ChatGPT subscription/OpenAI backend call was made from this PR branch.
  - No runtime-selection behavior was changed or live-tested.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints:

  The repro is a payload-level proof using the real OpenClaw request builder and provider stream entrypoint. It avoids sending account-backed ChatGPT traffic from a PR branch, so it proves the malformed outbound shape is fixed but does not prove the remote backend acceptance path end-to-end.

- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

  The pre-existing test in `src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts` was named:

  ```ts
  "does not replay Responses item ids for store-disabled ChatGPT requests"
  ```

  and explicitly expected the bad shape:

  ```ts
  expect(reasoningItem).toMatchObject({
    type: "reasoning",
    encrypted_content: "ciphertext",
    summary: [],
  });
  expect(reasoningItem).not.toHaveProperty("id");
  ```

  That made the regression worse by locking in `encrypted_content` without its `rs_*` item id. During this fix, updating that assertion before patching the shared native provider converter produced the exact failure shape:

  ```text
  AssertionError: expected { type: 'reasoning', …(2) } to match object { type: 'reasoning', …(3) }

  - Expected
  + Received

    {
      "encrypted_content": "ciphertext",
  -   "id": "rs_prior",
      "summary": [],
      "type": "reasoning",
    }
  ```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
npx pnpm@latest install --frozen-lockfile
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts -t "preserves encrypted reasoning item ids" --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t "preserves encrypted reasoning ids|keeps embedded replay provenance and encrypted reasoning ids|omits native Codex replay item ids" --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --reporter=dot
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Updated `src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts` so the native ChatGPT provider payload capture now requires encrypted reasoning replay to keep `id: "rs_prior"`.
- Updated `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` so store-disabled replay omits ordinary item ids but preserves encrypted reasoning item ids.
- Updated embedded replay-provenance fallback coverage to keep the reasoning item id with ciphertext.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The native ChatGPT provider test previously expected the malformed shape and passed while reinforcing bad behavior.
- After changing the assertion first, the captured payload failed exactly because it still had `encrypted_content` but no `id` until `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts` was fixed too.

If no test was added, why not?

Not applicable.

## Regression notes

This looks like a post-#88451 regression candidate rather than a Pi runtime-selection bug:

- #88451 moved/unified the old Codex/OpenAI naming (`openai-codex-responses` → `openai-chatgpt-responses`).
- The current `openai-chatgpt-responses` path combines:
  - `store: false`
  - `include: ["reasoning.encrypted_content"]`
  - `replayResponsesItemIds: false`
- That combination was stripping ids from all replay items, including encrypted reasoning items.
- The fix keeps the store-disabled no-id policy for ordinary items while avoiding the bad `encrypted_content`-without-`id` replay shape.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Multi-turn native ChatGPT/Codex Responses sessions should stop sending malformed encrypted reasoning replay payloads.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No auth or secret handling changes. Network payload shape changes only for encrypted reasoning replay items already being replayed.

What is the highest-risk area?

Preserving a Responses item id in `store=false` requests could interact with backend validation.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is intentionally narrow: it preserves ids only when `encrypted_content` is also present, because OpenAI's stateless replay model expects the prior output reasoning item to be replayed as a coherent item. Ordinary message and function-call ids are still omitted under `store=false`.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer/ClawSweeper review. If live ChatGPT subscription proof is required, the next step is a redacted live two-turn check from an account-backed environment.

Is the PR ready for review, draft, or blocked?

Ready for review.

Known follow-ups?

None for this PR. A separate follow-up could improve retry logging to distinguish stale encrypted replay from malformed encrypted replay, but this PR keeps scope focused on the payload bug.
